questions.question: The sudden ____ of the ecosystem was attributed to the introduction of an invasive species that outcompeted local predators.

options.A: deterioration
options.B: mitigation
options.C: subjugation
options.D: emancipation
options.E: rectification

questions.question: Despite the evidence suggesting a decline in literacy rates, some scholars ____ that digital platforms have simply shifted the medium of reading.

options.A: contend
options.B: renounce
options.C: refute
options.D: deplete
options.E: abdicate

questions.question: Advancements in nanotechnology have provided ____ benefits to the field of medical diagnostics, allowing for earlier detection of diseases.

options.A: unprecedented
options.B: ambiguous
options.C: redundant
options.D: precarious
options.E: obsolete

questions.question: Although the government has implemented several fiscal reforms to curb inflation, ____.

options.A: the central bank remains optimistic about the future of the currency
options.B: the purchasing power of the average citizen continues to decline
options.C: it was successful in reducing the national debt significantly
options.D: foreign investors have expressed a keen interest in the local market
options.E: because the agricultural sector has seen a record harvest this year

questions.question: ____ as long as scientists can find a sustainable way to store the energy produced by solar panels.

options.A: The global dependence on fossil fuels will likely persist
options.B: Renewable energy sources will become the primary power supply
options.C: The cost of electricity is expected to rise sharply next year
options.D: Environmental regulations are being loosened in many regions
options.E: Few people believe that climate change is a pressing issue

questions.question: While many ancient civilizations developed complex writing systems, ____.

options.A: the Incas used a system of knotted strings called quipus to keep records
options.B: their architectural achievements were equally impressive
options.C: which allowed them to document their history and laws
options.D: they were often located near major river valleys for irrigation
options.E: the study of archaeology has revealed much about their daily lives

questions.question: Because the results of the initial clinical trials were inconclusive, ____.

options.A: the pharmaceutical company decided to proceed with mass production
options.B: researchers are now planning a more extensive follow-up study
options.C: the drug has been approved for immediate use by the FDA
options.D: patients reported a significant improvement in their symptoms
options.E: the scientists were awarded a prestigious grant for their work

questions.question: Cloze Text (1/5): The Renaissance was a period of profound cultural change (8) ____ began in Italy and spread throughout Europe.

options.A: who
options.B: which
options.C: where
options.D: whom
options.E: whose

questions.question: Cloze Text (2/5): It was characterized by a (9) ____ of interest in the classical philosophy and art of ancient Greece and Rome.

options.A: revival
options.B: rejection
options.C: dismissal
options.D: restriction
options.E: suspension

questions.question: Cloze Text (3/5): During this era, many artists and thinkers began to (10) ____ emphasis on humanism and individual potential.

options.A: place
options.B: take
options.C: give
options.D: hold
options.E: keep

questions.question: Cloze Text (4/5): (11) ____, the invention of the printing press allowed these new ideas to circulate more rapidly than ever before.

options.A: However
options.B: Instead
options.C: Consequently
options.D: Moreover
options.E: Otherwise

questions.question: Cloze Text (5/5): This period eventually paved the way (12) ____ the modern scientific and philosophical developments of the Enlightenment.

options.A: for
options.B: to
options.C: with
options.D: at
options.E: by

questions.question: Modern tıp, genetik araştırmalar sayesinde hastalıkların teşhis ve tedavisinde devrim yaratmıştır.

options.A: Modern medicine has revolutionized the diagnosis and treatment of diseases thanks to genetic research.
options.B: Genetic research is the reason why modern medicine has changed the diagnosis of diseases.
options.C: The treatment of diseases has been revolutionized by modern medicine and genetic studies.
options.D: Thanks to genetic research, modern medicine is diagnosing and treating diseases more effectively.
options.E: Modern medicine and genetic research have worked together to revolutionize disease treatment.

questions.question: Küresel ısınma sadece buzulların erimesine neden olmakla kalmıyor, aynı zamanda aşırı hava olaylarının sıklığını da artırıyor.

options.A: Global warming causes the melting of glaciers and also the frequency of extreme weather events.
options.B: Not only does global warming cause glaciers to melt, but it also increases the frequency of extreme weather events.
options.C: Global warming is increasing extreme weather events while glaciers are melting simultaneously.
options.D: The melting of glaciers and the increase in extreme weather events are both results of global warming.
options.E: Besides melting glaciers, global warming is known for causing various extreme weather events.

questions.question: The rise of artificial intelligence has sparked intense debates regarding its ethical implications and impact on the labor market.

options.A: Yapay zekanın yükselişi, etik sonuçları ve iş gücü piyasası üzerindeki etkisi konusunda yoğun tartışmaları tetikledi.
options.B: Yapay zeka geliştikçe, etik sorunlar ve iş gücü piyasası daha fazla tartışılmaya başlandı.
options.C: İş gücü piyasası ve etik değerler, yapay zekanın yükselişiyle birlikte tartışma konusu oldu.
options.D: Yapay zekanın etik etkileri, iş dünyasında büyük tartışmalara yol açan ana unsurdur.
options.E: Yapay zeka üzerine yapılan tartışmalar genellikle etik ve iş gücü piyasası odaklıdır.

questions.question: It is widely believed that the success of a country's economy is closely linked to the quality of its education system.

options.A: Eğitim sisteminin kalitesiyle bir ülkenin ekonomisinin başarısı arasında bir bağ olduğu düşünülmektedir.
options.B: Bir ülkenin ekonomi başarısının, eğitim sisteminin kalitesine sıkı sıkıya bağlı olduğuna yaygın olarak inanılmaktadır.
options.C: Ülke ekonomisinin başarısı için eğitim sisteminin kalitesinin artırılması gerektiğine inanılır.
options.D: Yaygın inanışa göre, eğitim kalitesi bir ülkenin ekonomik başarısını doğrudan belirler.
options.E: Eğitim ve ekonomi arasındaki bağın, bir ülkenin gelişimi için kritik olduğu düşünülmektedir.

questions.question: The manager decided to postpone the meeting until next week because several key team members were unavailable.

options.A: Due to the absence of some important staff, the meeting was rescheduled for the following week by the manager.
options.B: The manager will cancel the meeting if the key team members cannot attend next week.
options.C: Unless the key team members show up, the manager won't hold the meeting next week.
options.D: The meeting was held last week because the manager knew the team would be busy this week.
options.E: Key team members requested the manager to delay the meeting until they were available.

questions.question: Rarely has a scientific discovery had such an immediate and profound impact on our understanding of the universe.

options.A: It is common for scientific discoveries to change how we see the universe quickly.
options.B: Scientific discoveries usually take a long time to affect our understanding of the cosmos.
options.C: This scientific discovery is unique because it changed our view of the universe very deeply and instantly.
options.D: Our understanding of the universe has been changed by many discoveries, but this one is the most important.
options.E: No other discovery in science has ever influenced our knowledge of the universe as much as this one.

questions.question: If urban planners had accounted for the rapid population growth, the city's current traffic congestion could have been avoided.

options.A: The city has traffic problems now because urban planners failed to foresee how quickly the population would grow.
options.B: Urban planners are trying to solve the traffic problem by limiting the population growth in the city.
options.C: Because the population grew so fast, urban planners were unable to prevent the traffic congestion.
options.D: The current traffic issues would not exist if the population had not grown as fast as the planners expected.
options.E: Urban planners predicted the population growth, but they could not avoid the traffic congestion.

questions.question: Despite the harsh weather conditions, the mountaineers managed to reach the summit after five days of climbing.

options.A: The mountaineers reached the top in five days, even though the weather was very bad.
options.B: The climb took five days because the weather conditions were extremely harsh for the mountaineers.
options.C: If the weather hadn't been so harsh, the mountaineers would have reached the summit sooner than five days.
options.D: The mountaineers were unable to reach the summit because of the harsh weather during their five-day climb.
options.E: After five days of bad weather, the mountaineers gave up on reaching the summit.

questions.question:
Arthur: Have you seen the latest reports on the melting of the Arctic ice caps? 

 Brenda: Yes, it's quite alarming. The rate of melting is much faster than previously predicted. 

 Arthur: ____ 

 Brenda: Exactly. This could lead to a significant rise in sea levels, threatening coastal cities worldwide.

options.A: Do you think governments are doing enough to stop it?
options.B: It seems our current climate models might need to be revised.
options.C: The ice caps have always melted during the summer months.
options.D: Is there any way to reverse the damage already done?
options.E: I heard that some species are actually benefiting from the warmer water.

questions.question:
Doctor: Your blood tests show a high level of cholesterol, which increases your risk of heart disease. 

 Patient: I see. I've been trying to eat healthy, but I guess it hasn't been enough. 

 Doctor: ____ 

 Patient: That sounds reasonable. I'll start incorporating those changes into my daily routine immediately.

options.A: You should consider taking medication if your diet doesn't improve.
options.B: In addition to diet, regular physical activity is crucial for lowering cholesterol.
options.C: Many people struggle with their cholesterol levels despite their best efforts.
options.D: Heart disease is the leading cause of death in many developed countries.
options.E: We will need to run more tests to see if there are any other underlying issues.

questions.question:
Interviewer: Your resume is impressive, but we're looking for someone with more leadership experience. 

 Candidate: I understand. However, in my previous role, I frequently led project teams and mentored junior staff. 

 Interviewer: ____ 

 Candidate: Certainly. For instance, I spearheaded a team of five to develop a new software tool that improved our efficiency by 20%.

options.A: That's good to hear, but we need someone with a formal management title.
options.B: Could you provide some specific examples of your leadership in those situations?
options.C: What did your colleagues think of your leadership style during those projects?
options.D: We have many candidates with similar experiences, so what makes you stand out?
options.E: Why didn't you include those details in your initial application?

questions.question:
Student: Professor, I'm struggling to find enough primary sources for my research paper on the French Revolution. 

 Professor: Have you tried looking through the digital archives of the National Library? 

 Student: ____ 

 Professor: In that case, I recommend visiting the university library's special collections department.

options.A: Yes, but most of the documents there are in French, which I don't speak fluently.
options.B: No, I didn't know they had a digital archive. I'll check it out today.
options.C: I've already exhausted those resources and still need more specific data.
options.D: Is it possible to use secondary sources instead of primary ones for this paper?
options.E: The National Library is currently closed for renovations, unfortunately.

questions.question: The concept of sustainable development emerged in the late 20th century as a response to growing concerns about environmental degradation. ____. This definition emphasizes the need to balance economic growth with environmental protection and social equity. To achieve this, governments and organizations must adopt long-term strategies that minimize resource depletion.

options.A: It is often defined as development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.
options.B: Many critics argue that sustainable development is an unattainable goal in a capitalist global economy.
options.C: The Industrial Revolution led to a massive increase in the consumption of natural resources worldwide.
options.D: Environmental groups have been advocating for stricter regulations on carbon emissions for decades.
options.E: However, the primary focus of most developing nations remains on rapid industrialization and poverty reduction.

questions.question: Psychologists have long studied the effects of sleep deprivation on cognitive function. Research indicates that lack of sleep can impair attention, memory, and decision-making skills. ____. For example, staying awake for 24 hours can lead to a level of impairment comparable to that of alcohol intoxication.

options.A: The recommended amount of sleep for an average adult is between seven and nine hours per night.
options.B: Sleep disorders such as insomnia and sleep apnea are becoming increasingly common in modern society.
options.C: The severity of these impairments often correlates with the duration of the sleep loss.
options.D: Many people use caffeine as a temporary way to mask the symptoms of tiredness.
options.E: Dreams occur primarily during the REM stage of the sleep cycle.

questions.question: In the field of linguistics, the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis suggests that the language a person speaks influences how they perceive the world. ____. Others, however, advocate for a weaker version, known as linguistic relativity, which suggests that language merely shapes rather than determines thought.

options.A: This idea has been the subject of much debate among cognitive scientists and anthropologists.
options.B: Some proponents of the hypothesis argue for linguistic determinism, the idea that language dictates thought.
options.C: Languages differ significantly in their grammatical structures and vocabulary across different cultures.
options.D: Learning a second language can provide individuals with a new perspective on their own culture.
options.E: Most modern linguists have moved away from the more extreme interpretations of this hypothesis.

questions.question: The Great Depression was the most severe economic downturn in the history of the industrialized world. It began after the stock market crash of October 1929 and lasted until the late 1930s. ____. Millions of people lost their jobs, and banks across the United States failed, leading to a massive loss of savings.

options.A: The New Deal programs introduced by President Roosevelt helped to stimulate the economy.
options.B: During this period, international trade declined sharply as countries imposed high tariffs.
options.C: The social and political effects of the Depression were felt globally, not just in America.
options.D: The unemployment rate in the U.S. reached an unprecedented 25 percent at its peak.
options.E: World War II is often credited with finally bringing the Great Depression to an end.

questions.question: Hardly any of the candidates were qualified for the demanding position in the international firm.

options.A: Almost all the candidates had the necessary skills for the job at the international company.
options.B: Only a very small number of the applicants possessed the qualifications required for the difficult role.
options.C: None of the candidates who applied for the position were considered suitable for the firm.
options.D: The international firm was looking for many qualified candidates, but they only found a few.
options.E: Many candidates were qualified, but the position at the international firm was too demanding for them.

questions.question: The discovery of the new vaccine was so significant that it overshadowed all other medical news that year.

options.A: The new vaccine was important, but there were other more significant medical news stories that year.
options.B: No other medical news was as important as the discovery of the new vaccine during that year.
options.C: The vaccine discovery was one of the many important medical breakthroughs reported that year.
options.D: Because of the new vaccine, other medical news was not reported at all by the media.
options.E: The medical community was so focused on the vaccine that they ignored other important developments.

questions.question: It is not until you travel abroad that you truly appreciate the uniqueness of your own culture.

options.A: Traveling abroad helps you understand other cultures, but not your own.
options.B: You can only really value the distinctiveness of your own culture after you have visited other countries.
options.C: If you don't travel abroad, you will never be able to appreciate any culture at all.
options.D: People who travel frequently tend to value their own culture more than those who stay at home.
options.E: Appreciating your own culture is a prerequisite for understanding the cultures of other nations.

questions.question: (I) The Industrial Revolution marked a major turning point in history, influencing almost every aspect of daily life. (II) It began in Great Britain in the late 18th century and later spread to other parts of the world. (III) During this time, the transition from hand production methods to machines revolutionized manufacturing. (IV) Many people moved from rural areas to cities in search of work in the new factories. (V) Ancient Rome also had a sophisticated system of roads and aqueducts that supported its urban population.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Solar energy is one of the most promising sources of renewable energy available today. (II) It involves capturing sunlight and converting it into electricity using photovoltaic cells. (III) Unlike fossil fuels, solar energy does not produce harmful greenhouse gas emissions. (IV) The cost of installing solar panels has decreased significantly over the past decade. (V) Nuclear power plants, however, generate a large amount of electricity with minimal carbon output.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) The human brain is a complex organ that controls all functions of the body. (II) It is composed of billions of neurons that communicate through electrical and chemical signals. (III) Different regions of the brain are responsible for specific tasks, such as memory and movement. (IV) Regular exercise is known to improve cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of heart disease. (V) Protecting the brain from injury is essential for maintaining overall health and well-being.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: Passage: The Great Barrier Reef, located off the coast of Australia, is the world's largest coral reef system. It is composed of over 2,900 individual reefs and 900 islands stretching for over 2,300 kilometers. The reef supports a wide diversity of life, including many vulnerable or endangered species. However, the reef faces significant threats from climate change, particularly coral bleaching caused by rising ocean temperatures. When water is too warm, corals expel the algae living in their tissues, causing them to turn completely white. This process can be fatal if the high temperatures persist. Question: According to the passage, what is the primary cause of coral bleaching in the Great Barrier Reef?

options.A: The introduction of invasive species into the reef system.
options.B: Overfishing and the destruction of natural habitats.
options.C: An increase in the temperature of the ocean water.
options.D: Pollution from nearby coastal cities and industries.
options.E: The physical damage caused by tourism and boat traffic.

questions.question: Passage: The Great Barrier Reef... (same as above) Question: Which of the following is true about the coral bleaching process mentioned in the text?

options.A: It is a natural process that helps corals reproduce more effectively.
options.B: It occurs when corals absorb too much algae from the surrounding water.
options.C: Corals can survive bleaching if the water temperature returns to normal quickly.
options.D: The process is always fatal for corals, regardless of the water temperature.
options.E: It only affects the 900 islands within the Great Barrier Reef system.

questions.question: Passage: The Great Barrier Reef... (same as above) Question: What can be inferred about the diversity of life in the Great Barrier Reef?

options.A: It is mostly composed of species that are not found anywhere else on Earth.
options.B: The survival of many at-risk species depends on the health of the reef.
options.C: The diversity is decreasing because the reef is too large to support all species.
options.D: Most of the species living there are resistant to the effects of climate change.
options.E: It is primarily made up of algae rather than coral or fish.

questions.question: Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the 18th century, transformed the world's economies from agrarian to industrial. This shift was driven by technological innovations like the steam engine and the spinning jenny. While it led to unprecedented economic growth and urbanization, it also brought about harsh working conditions and significant social inequality. Workers often labored for long hours in dangerous environments for low wages. In response, labor unions began to form to advocate for better pay and safer conditions. Question: What was a major driver of the shift from agrarian to industrial economies during the Industrial Revolution?

options.A: The discovery of new trade routes to Asia and the Americas.
options.B: A sudden increase in the global population and demand for food.
options.C: Technological advancements such as the steam engine.
options.D: The abolition of labor unions and workers' rights.
options.E: The migration of workers from cities back to rural farms.

questions.question: Passage: The Industrial Revolution... (same as above) Question: What does the text suggest about the social consequences of the Industrial Revolution?

options.A: It immediately led to an equal distribution of wealth among all citizens.
options.B: Working conditions were generally safe and beneficial for most laborers.
options.C: The movement toward urbanization was a slow and gradual process.
options.D: It created challenges like social inequality and poor working conditions.
options.E: Labor unions were formed by the government to control the workers.

questions.question: Passage: The Industrial Revolution... (same as above) Question: Why did labor unions emerge during this period, according to the passage?

options.A: To encourage more people to move to rural areas for work.
options.B: To help factory owners increase their production efficiency.
options.C: To fight for improved wages and safer work environments for laborers.
options.D: To promote the use of traditional hand production methods.
options.E: To manage the transition from steam power to electricity.
